key_insight: DeepSpot-M is a multimodal foundation model that turns histology into transcriptome-wide virtual spatial transcriptomics by querying genes with learned embeddings, including genes not seen during training.
clinical_relevance: It could make spatial molecular profiling scalable on routine pathology slides, helping map tumor biology and discover biomarkers across many cancers without requiring every patient to undergo costly spatial assays.
method: Multimodal gene-query model built from foundation embeddings spanning DNA, RNA, proteins, single cells, and biomedical text.
Trained on a large pan-cancer dataset to predict spatial expression across the protein-coding transcriptome, including unseen genes.
Supports test-time adaptation and additional tasks such as transcriptome restoration, cross-species non-coding RNA inference, and variant-effect mapping.
evidence_signal: According to the abstract, it transferred to held-out cancers, outperformed specialized models trained on those cancers, and was used to generate a virtual atlas of 28,664 TCGA slides across 32 cancer types.
watch_item: Performance and utility may vary across staining quality, tissue types, and cohorts, so real-world deployment will need careful external validation and workflow integration.
paper_title: DeepSpot-M: a multimodal foundation model for transcriptome-wide virtual spatial transcriptomics from histology
